subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Analyse the poem 'A Concrete Example' using literary and critical perspectives
Evaluate how humour, irony, and imagery contribute to the meaning of the poem
Interpret the symbolism and wordplay in the title
Develop analytical and interpretative reading skills
Strengthen vocabulary, grammar, and poetic analysis skills

lesson_plan.title: Understanding the Title and Context
topics_covered: Literal and symbolic meanings of the word 'concrete'
Introduction to the poet and poem
Context of gardens and aesthetic perspectives
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of wordplay and pun
student_activities: Discuss multiple meanings of the word 'concrete'
Predict the theme of the poem from the title

lesson_plan.title: Close Reading of the Poem
student_activities: Annotate important lines in the poem
Identify phrases that create vivid images

lesson_plan.title: Character and Perspective Analysis
topics_covered: Contrast between the speaker’s view and Mrs. Jones’s perspective
Exploring the idea of subjective beauty
student_activities: Write a short character analysis of Mrs. Jones
Discuss how different people perceive beauty differently

lesson_plan.title: Literary Devices and Tone
topics_covered: Humour and satire in the poem
Situational irony in the final lines
Alliteration, rhyme scheme, and poetic rhythm
student_activities: Identify examples of alliteration and rhyme
Explain how the ending creates irony and humour

lesson_plan.title: Theme Interpretation and Language Practice
topics_covered: Theme of perspective and appreciation of uniqueness
student_activities: Write a reflective paragraph about the poem’s message
Complete word formation exercises from the chapter

teaching_aids: Visual images of different types of gardens
teacher_tip: Encourage students to interpret the poem from multiple perspectives. Discuss how humour and irony reveal the idea that beauty and value often depend on individual viewpoints.
